dependencies: Third-party pipelines and facilities interconnected to Williams' pipelines for natural gas and NGL transportation
Dependence on natural gas supplies from producers in supply basins
Reliance on third-party service providers for accounting and information technology services
Dependence on skilled labor, equipment, materials, permits for construction activities
risks: Construction cost overruns including due to inflation or tariffs on foreign-made materials and goods (including steel and steel pipes)
Supplier concentration risks from dependence on single or limited number of suppliers
Failure of service providers or disruptions to outsourcing relationships
Third-party pipeline unavailability could reduce capacity to transport or deliver products
Supply chain disruptions affecting capital project execution

regulatory_risks: EPA and state environmental regulations including air quality standards
Potential new laws restricting natural gas use in buildings or power generation
FERC regulations and rate case proceedings
Federal and state pipeline safety regulations
Cybersecurity regulations from TSA and Coast Guard
Rate case proceedings and potential rate refunds
FERC Updated Certificate Policy Statement uncertainty
Potential changes to depreciation rates through FERC rate cases
FERC regulation of interstate natural gas pipeline rates and operations
Climate Commitment Act of Washington State requiring emission allowances
New or revised emissions standards for volatile organic compounds and methane
Delays or denials of necessary permits for expansion projects
Natural Gas Act Section 5 rate adjustments
State regulations including Washington State Climate Commitment Act requiring carbon allowance purchases
Pipeline safety regulations under Natural Gas Pipeline Safety Act and PHMSA requirements
Environmental remediation obligations under CERCLA and state laws
Environmental laws and regulations including climate change and greenhouse gas emissions regulations
Gathering regulations by state authorities (Texas Railroad Commission, Louisiana, Ohio, New York)
Opposition to hydrocarbon-based energy development
State-level carbon pricing programs
Regulatory actions that could decrease tariff rates or increase costs
Environmental restoration and remediation requirements
Changes in government regulations or their interpretation affecting business operations
Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act requirements for offshore pipelines
Greenhouse gas emission regulations

sanction_risks: OPEC and other oil exporting nations' production controls affecting domestic production
Conflicts in Middle East affecting global financial markets and operations
Geopolitical turmoil in Middle East and Eastern Europe affecting commodity markets
Russian invasion of Ukraine creating uncertainty and potential operational impacts
Acts of terrorism and geopolitical conflicts potentially targeting energy infrastructure
